title: The Mermaid's Tear
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: A pearl diver in 1920s Indonesia discovers a pearl that glows with its own light and sets off a hunt involving pirates, colonial powers, and a legend.
long_description:
In 1924, deep in the Flores Sea of the Dutch East Indies, a young pearl diver named Suli brings up a pearl that glows with its own light. Word spreads across the archipelago, drawing a ruthless German treasure hunter, a conflicted local Raja caught between tradition and colonial power, and a band of Celebes pirates. As white sails appear on the horizon and competing claims collide, Suli must navigate treachery, legend, and the truth behind the Mermaid's Tear — a pearl said to be the frozen grief of a sea spirit who drowned for love.

From the burning of his village to a desperate flight across open water, Suli discovers that the pearl's true power lies not in its value but in the choice of what to keep and what to return to the sea. The novel explores themes of colonialism, greed, and the cost of legend against the vivid backdrop of the Indonesian archipelago.
main_characters: Suli — A pearl diver from Komodo Island who discovers the Mermaid's Tear
Captain Joachim Brandt — A German treasure hunter commanding the schooner Seeschlange
The Raja Alauddin — The Dutch-backed local ruler who sees the pearl as a symbol of independence
Ibrahim — Suli's elder brother, a pragmatic fisherman
Melati (Rajah Laut) — The pirate queen of the eastern passages
De Groot — The Dutch colonial agent on Flores
Tuan Khalid — An old pearl merchant who knows the legend
